QF.id: Role of RTAs in Mutual Fund Transactions_25

question.text: By what time must the NAV of most mutual fund schemes be uploaded on the AMFI website?

answer.text: 9 PM on the same business day
10 AM on the next business day (T+1)
3 PM on the same business day
11 PM on the same business day (T-day)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI mandates that NAV for most mutual fund schemes must be uploaded on the AMFI website and the mutual fund's own website by 11 PM on the same business day (T-day). **The Trap:** FoF and overseas fund schemes have a later deadline of 10 AM on T+1. Don't confuse the general 11 PM deadline with these exceptions. **Cross-Exam Context:** NAV publication timelines are tested across NISM Series V-A and II-B. The RTA plays a key role in computing and uploading NAV within these deadlines. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If you need to make investment decisions based on the day's NAV, check the AMFI website after 11 PM. For FoF schemes, you may need to wait until the next morning. Plan SIP timing discussions with clients accordingly.
